channel.title: Analysis: Introducing Automatic Platform Optimization, starting with WordPress
channel.description:
**Cloudflare has started a new service for WordPress sites to cache HTML from dynamic requests** (something similar as what Strattic offers)
https://blog.cloudflare.com/automatic-platform-optimizations-starting-with-wordpress/
I have already shared my opinion on this but I'd like to know what you think about it
https://community.frontity.org/t/automatic-platform-optimization-cloudflare/3070/3?u=juanma
I think at some point this could be a cache service we can rely for all the Frontity "modes"

item.title: Analysis: Introducing Automatic Platform Optimization, starting with WordPress
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Yeah, I think this is great. We should try it out at some point

Read full topic
item.source: Analysis: Introducing Automatic Platform Optimization, starting with WordPress

item.title: Analysis: Introducing Automatic Platform Optimization, starting with WordPress
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Cloudflare has started a new service for WordPress sites to cache HTML from dynamic requests (something similar as what Strattic offers)

The Cloudflare Blog – 2 Oct 20

Introducing Automatic Platform Optimization, starting with WordPress

Today, we are announcing a new service to serve more than just the static content of your website with the Automatic Platform Optimization (APO) service. With this launch, we are supporting WordPress, the most popular website hosting solution serving...

I have already shared my opinion on this but I’d like to know what you think about it

Automatic Platform Optimization Cloudflare 🙋Get Help
Hi @Kent, This is actually great news! With this service, HTML generated by WordPress can be also cached by this layer improving a lot the response time. This service is similar to what other companies are already providing like Strattic & Shifter This reinforces the idea we have that the performance of a site doesn’t really have to do with the platform chosen to create the site (Wordpress vs Gatsby vs Frontity, for example) but with a good cache strategy [Fly-Fest_2020_-Headless_WordPress(…

I think at some point this could be a cache service we can rely for all the Frontity “modes”

Read full topic
item.source: Analysis: Introducing Automatic Platform Optimization, starting with WordPress
